untranslatable_reason: This signal requires detection of: (1) existing covered call position with locked-in loss state, (2) support level breaks, and (3) important trend line breaks. These are portfolio/position-level constructs and technical chart patterns that cannot be expressed using only the allowed price/indicator-based operators. Support levels and trend lines require manual chart identification or multi-bar pattern logic not available in the allowed indicator set. The action ('roll down position') is also a portfolio management directive, not a price entry/exit signal.
translation_notes: MCMILLAN_DRY_32 is a risk management rule for covered call positions rather than a pure price-action entry/exit signal. It requires contextual knowledge of position P&L state and manual identification of support/resistance levels. To operationalize this in a backtest, you would need: (1) a position tracking module, (2) support/resistance detection algorithms (e.g., swing point identification), or (3) manual regime definition. As written, it is not translatable to indicator-only conditions.
